svg.title: Self-custody friendliness versus reporting burden
svg.desc: A two-by-two quadrant. The horizontal axis is self-custody friendliness, from low on the left to high on the right. The vertical axis is reporting burden, from low at the bottom to high at the top. Top-left: hard to self-custody and heavily reported. Top-right: easy to self-custody but heavily reported, which describes many developed economies moving toward broker reporting. Bottom-left: hard to self-custody but lightly reported. Bottom-right: easy to self-custody and lightly reported. No countries are plotted, because fit depends on your facts rather than a ranking.
svg.text: Friendly for whom? Two axes, no ranking
A country can be friendly for institutions and ambiguous for self-custody at the same time.
